notes: Frozen contract for the canonical `check verify eval <target> --format json` envelope. Two targets share the same envelope shape: `eval semantic <fixture>` (offline-fixtures safety) and `eval skills` (dry-run or explicit-runtime). Pin is structural: required keys, value types, and the small set of literal values (command='verify', family='eval', status union, safety mode union). Field content that legitimately varies by target/fixture (engine.command array elements, diagnostics stdout/stderr content, resolved safety.summary text) is not pinned. The fail path on --format json reuses the canonical invalid-input envelope (rc 2, status='fail', error.{class,message,valid_values?,syntax?,example?}); see packages/cli/test/cli/fixtures/oracle/invalid-input-envelope.json. The npm-cli-surface.json oracle (packages/cli/test/cli/fixtures/oracle/npm-cli-surface.json) covers the cross-cutting npm exit-code matrix.

skills_run.description: Out of scope for the default pinned shape — the live envelope is the same shape as the dry-run/explicit-runtime case but with `safety.live === true` and `engine.command` ending in `--runtime <runtime>` without `--dry-run`.

failEnvelope.description: Verify fail envelopes on --format json (unsupported family/target, eval skills with --run + --dry-run, etc.) reuse the canonical invalid-input envelope contract (rc 2, status='fail', error.{class,message,valid_values?,syntax?,example?}).
